title: Cineflux model and dashboard specification walkthrough
author: Marques Pita, Manuel
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Reception research needs going beyond ratings and sentiment. CineFlux provides a structured, interpretable account of how different audiences engage with cinema along moral and motivational dimensions. The system's technical deliverable is not designed to be read by the audience experts who will use it in practice. This walkthrough bridges that gap. Nine thematically bounded blocks move from the problem CineFlux addresses, namely why sentiment classifiers and aggregate ratings lose the psychological specificity that audience research requires, through the theoretical model (Moral Foundations and Motivational Orientation operationalised as reception variables), the LLM-based annotation pipeline (Trigger–Appraisal–Stance extraction), the corpus and cohort scoring architecture, profile reading, divergence analysis, and a field-by-field reference for the tool interface and JSON output schema. A final block documents the MVP's current design and limitations: data scope, model coverage, pipeline validation status, and the interpretive conditions under which outputs should be treated with caution. Each block is self-contained and readable in isolation; read in sequence, they accumulate into a working mental model of the system precise enough to generate, interrogate, and appropriately caveat CineFlux findings in research and policy contexts.
